abstract: Comprehensive authoring guide for the Swirls DSL. Covers file structure (thirteen top-level declarations plus the optional `version:` line: schema, form, webhook, schedule, workflow, stream, trigger, secret, auth, postgres, disk, agent, channel), workflow declarations (the keyword is `workflow`; `graph` is a legacy alias), all 16 node types (agent, ai, bucket, code, disk, email, http, map, parallel, postgres, scrape, stream, switch, wait, while, workflow), inline `subgraph { }` blocks for map/while, embedded TypeScript/JSON/SQL blocks, the context object including `context.iteration.*` (item/index/input/previous), resources, triggers, versioned top-level stream blocks (a `version` pointer plus a `versions:` map carrying per-version schema/condition/prepare) read by version-pinned `type: stream` nodes, reusable top-level `schema` blocks referenced by bare identifier, form `visibility` (public/internal), webhook shared-secret authentication via `secret:` + `header:`, agent blocks with tools, roles, and subagent `team` delegation, `channel` blocks binding an agent to Slack / Linear / Discord / web, reviews, failure policies, and known parser pitfalls with an exhaustive validator-diagnostic cheatsheet.
